body:
Refactor `manual_sparse_jacobian.jl` to organize tracer lists and jacobian blocks by physical process (advection, sedimentation, diffusion, SGS transport), with centralized process-based tracer-name utilities and automated block assembly, while keeping the exact same sparsity pattern and numerical values.

## What changed

New file `src/utils/tracer_processes.jl` — the central, process-based tracer classification:
- `sedimenting_tracer_names(Y)`, `sedimenting_mass_names(Y)`, `diffused_gs_scalar_names(Y)`, `advected_gs_scalar_names(Y)`, `microphysics_tracer_names(Y)` for the grid scale, and `sedimenting_sgs_tracer_names(Y)`, `advected_sgs_scalar_names(Y)`, `passive_sgs_tracer_names(Y)` for the updrafts. All lists filter candidate names by what's actually in Y, so a new tracer opts into its processes automatically.

- Per-tracer process properties: `sedimentation_velocity_name (e.g. ρq_rai → ᶜwᵣ)`, `condensate_phase` (returns `TD.Liquid()` / `TD.Ice()`), and phase-dispatched internal_energy_function, `condensate_e_int_offset`, `condensate_cv_difference`. They replace the hardcoded and duplicated `(name, e_int, ∂cv∂q)`.

`manual_sparse_jacobian.jl` rewritten around processes:

- `jacobian_cache` now assembles the matrix from per-process block builders (`advection_jacobian_blocks`, `diffusion_jacobian_blocks`, `sedimentation_jacobian_blocks`, `sgs_advection_jacobian_blocks`, `sgs_massflux_jacobian_blocks`). Blocks requested by multiple processes are de-duplicated by `merge_jacobian_blocks`, and a final `fallback_identity_blocks` pass gives every state variable without a diagonal a `-I` block. That fallback eliminated all the `ρq_gas_A_if_available`-style special cases. A passive tracer now needs no mention in this file at all (!)

- The 900-line `update_jacobian!` is split into per-process functions mirroring `implicit_tendency!`: `update_advection_jacobian!`, `update_sedimentation_jacobian!`, `update_diffusion_jacobian!`, and `update_sgs_{advection,diffusion,entr_detr,boundary_condition,massflux}_jacobian!`. The scratch-space and block-initialization dependencies between them are documented in an ordering contract in `update_jacobian!`; eddy diffusivities are now computed once and passed to both diffusion updates.

- Removed unused `ᶜkappa_mʲ` computation and a few unused constants. Left TODO comments where the Jacobian intentionally diverges from the tendencies: SGS entrainment skips number concentrations; SGS diffusion skips passive tracers.
title: Process-based jacobian refactor

body: In the words of Mr. Claude: >The SGS-quadrature 1-moment microphysics broadcast (set_microphysics_tendency_cache!, PrognosticEDMFX path) is register-bound, and much of its cost is fixed per grid point rather than in the quadrature loop. It recomputed several quantities inline at every point: the lazy environment inputs (air_density and the specific_env_value reconstructions for q_lcl/q_icl/q_rai/q_sno), and the quadrature-invariant liquid fraction and linearized saturation-excess mean. > >Materialize those into scratch fields once (in cheap, high-occupancy kernels) and pass the precomputed λ and mu_S into microphysics_tendencies_1m, so the heavy kernel reads them instead of recomputing them. Numerically identical; large speedup on the AMIP 1M prognostic-EDMF config (env microphysics kernel roughly halved). This is an 11% speedup for the flagship AMIP (prog. EDMF 1M), though it's kind of hard to test because of dependency conflicts.
title: Precompute quadrature-invariant microphysics inputs to cut kernel cost
